_doc: Autopilot backlog. The planner reads this file and picks the next eligible task. Tasks have status (pending, in_progress, completed, blocked), priority (1-100, higher first), kind (self-mod, agent-author, reflect, audit, maintenance), payload (the body to POST to the target signal). The autopilot fires target_signal_id at target_workspace_id with signal_payload as the {payload: ...} body.

signal_payload.task_brief: Implement MdMemoryAdapter facade in packages/adapters-md/. The MemoryAdapter interface (packages/agent-sdk/src/memory-adapter.ts lines 186-200) has 5 methods: corpus, list, bootstrap, history, rollback. Implement: corpus() (narrative kind only - throws NotImplementedError for retrieval/dedup/kv), list() (scan disk for narrative corpora), bootstrap() (render narrative corpus contents as markdown for system prompt injection - empty string if no corpora), history()/rollback() throw NotImplementedError. Storage: {root}/memory/{workspaceId}/narrative/{corpusName}.md. Reuse MdNarrativeCorpus internally. 5 vitest tests. Mirror md-narrative-corpus.ts conventions.

tasks.title: Add step_route to grilled_xylem execute-self-mod-task FSM

signal_payload.task_brief: Update friday-starter/fast-self-mod/workspace.yml to add a new state step_route at the start of execute-self-mod-task FSM. step_route calls task-router (type: user, agent: task-router) with task_brief and target_files from the signal. If the result.route == 'quick-fix', transition directly to step_implement (skipping step_research). Otherwise transition to step_research as today. Also add task-router as an agent under agents:, type user, agent: task-router. Add a routing-result documentType matching the task-router output shape (route, rationale, estimated_files_changed). The FSM change must preserve all existing transitions. Reviewer must verify the agent ID 'task-router' is referenced in the FSM (closing the orphan-agent gap).

signal_payload.agent_brief: See /tmp/skill-author-brief.json — the autopilot will fetch this brief from the operator's drafts. For now, this is a placeholder; the brief is in flight.

Custom Python WASM agent. Lists user agents from GET /api/agents (filtered to type=user), GET /api/workspaces for all loaded workspaces, GET /api/workspaces/{id} for each to get the parsed config, scans config.agents and config.jobs.*.fsm for `agent: <id>` references, returns list of orphans (registered user agents with no consumer FSM reference). Output: { total_user_agents, referenced_count, orphan_count, orphans: [{agent_id, version}], rationale }. Pure deterministic, HTTP only, no LLM. Mirror agents/skill-publisher/agent.py for HTTP-only pattern. Closes the cross-artifact integration gap that the single-session reflector cannot see.

tasks.title: Wire MemoryAdapter.bootstrap() into runtime.ts session creation

signal_payload.task_brief: Modify packages/workspace/src/runtime.ts to call MemoryAdapter.bootstrap(workspaceId, agentId) at session start and prepend the result to the agent's system prompt. Behind a feature flag (env var ATLAS_MEMORY_BOOTSTRAP=true) so it doesn't break existing flows. Only activates if a memory adapter is configured. Use MdMemoryAdapter from @atlas/adapters-md. Add an integration test that verifies the bootstrap text appears in the prompt.
